Goal: Build the Weekly Commit Module v1 — a production micro-frontend (TypeScript/React) with Java 21 modular-monolith backend replacing 15Five for weekly planning. Implements structured weekly commitments with RCDO strategy linkage, chess-layer prioritization, lock/reconcile lifecycle with immutable snapshots, explicit carry-forward with provenance, native tickets with status workflow, manager team dashboard with exception review, conservative explainable AI assistance, rules-based notifications, derived read models for reporting, and comprehensive audit logging. Delivered as a route-level Module Federation remote integrated into the PA host app. Note: The PRD recommends Java 21 (§22) and all checks are npm-based; the monorepo root package.json orchestrates both the TypeScript frontend (Vite/React) and Java backend (Gradle) via shell-out scripts. SQL migrations use Flyway against PostgreSQL.

## Original plan overview:
{
  "totalSteps": 20,
  "stepTitles": [
    "1. Monorepo scaffolding and build infrastructure",
    "2. Domain model types, enums, and database schema",
    "3. RCDO hierarchy management API",
    "4. Weekly plan creation and commit CRUD API",
    "5. Lock lifecycle and baseline snapshot API",
    "6. Post-lock scope change and reconciliation API",
    "7. Carry-forward and ticket/work item API",
    "8. Team weekly view, manager review, and permissions API",
    "9. Frontend micro-frontend setup and routing",
    "10. RCDO management UI",
    "11. My Week page with commit list and capacity meter",
    "12. Lock flow and reconcile view UI",
    "13. Team Week dashboard UI",
    "14. Tickets UI and search/filter/history views",
    "15. Notifications backend and frontend panel",
    "16. AI provider abstraction and commit assistance",
    "17. AI risk detection and reconciliation assistance",
    "18. Derived read models and operational reporting",
    "19. Configuration management and admin settings",
    "20. Audit logging, accessibility, and integration polish"
  ]
}

## Completed steps — DO NOT change these, they are already done:
1. [DONE] Monorepo scaffolding and build infrastructure (step-1)
2. [DONE] Domain model types, enums, and database schema (step-2)
3. [DONE] RCDO hierarchy management API (step-3)
4. [DONE] Weekly plan creation and commit CRUD API (step-4)
5. [DONE] Lock lifecycle and baseline snapshot API (step-5)
6. [DONE] Post-lock scope change and reconciliation API (step-6)
7. [DONE] Carry-forward and ticket/work item API (step-7)
8. [DONE] Team weekly view, manager review, and permissions API (step-8)
9. [DONE] Frontend micro-frontend setup and routing (step-9)
10. [DONE] RCDO management UI (step-10)
11. [DONE] My Week page with commit list and capacity meter (step-11)
12. [DONE] Lock flow and reconcile view UI (step-12)
13. [DONE] Team Week dashboard UI (step-13)

## Why the current step requires replanning (escalation reason):
Step 14 frontend was built against APIs/data shapes that the current backend does not provide, so the feature set cannot work in runtime without broader contract-alignment work.

## Current step 14 and remaining steps that need replanning:
14. Tickets UI and search/filter/history views (step-14)
15. Notifications backend and frontend panel (step-15)
16. AI provider abstraction and commit assistance (step-16)
17. AI risk detection and reconciliation assistance (step-17)
18. Derived read models and operational reporting (step-18)
19. Configuration management and admin settings (step-19)
20. Audit logging, accessibility, and integration polish (step-20)

Your task: revise the plan from step 14 onward to address the escalation reason.
Write the complete updated plan (including the completed prefix unchanged) as JSON to .pi/duet/runs/2026-03-24T03-49-04-151Z-dd75a832/draft-plan.json
Use the write tool to create the file.

Important: Do NOT change steps 1–13 (they are already completed). Only revise step 14 onward.

  "goal": "Build the Weekly Commit Module v1 — a production micro-frontend (TypeScript/React) with Java 21 modular-monolith backend replacing 15Five for weekly planning. Implements structured weekly commitments with RCDO strategy linkage, chess-layer prioritization, lock/reconcile lifecycle with immutable snapshots, explicit carry-forward with provenance, native tickets with status workflow, manager team dashboard with exception review, conservative explainable AI assistance, rules-based notifications, derived read models for reporting, and comprehensive audit logging. Delivered as a route-level Module Federation remote integrated into the PA host app. Note: The PRD recommends Java 21 (§22) and all checks are npm-based; the monorepo root package.json orchestrates both the TypeScript frontend (Vite/React) and Java backend (Gradle) via shell-out scripts. SQL migrations use Flyway against PostgreSQL.",
  "steps": [
    {
      "id": "step-1",
      "title": "Monorepo scaffolding and build infrastructure",
      "description": "Create a polyglot monorepo at the project root.

**Root:** `package.json` with npm scripts that orchestrate both frontend and backend: `lint` (ESLint for TS + Spotless/Checkstyle for Java), `typecheck` (tsc --noEmit on frontend/shared), `test` (Vitest for TS + Gradle test for Java), `build` (Vite build for frontend + Gradle build for backend).

**frontend/**: Scaffold a Vite + React 18 + TypeScript (strict mode) project. Configure `tsconfig.json` with strict: true, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, and exactOptionalPropertyTypes. Add ESLint with @typescript-eslint, Prettier, and Vitest. Create a minimal App.tsx placeholder. Add a trivial passing test.

**backend/**: Scaffold a Spring Boot 3.2+ / Java 21 project using Gradle (Kotlin DSL). Configure `build.gradle.kts` with Spring Web, Spring Data JPA, Flyway, PostgreSQL driver, JUnit 5, and Spotless (google-java-format). Create the main application class and a trivial health controller with a passing test. Add `src/main/resources/application.yml` with embedded H2 for tests.

**shared/**: Create a TypeScript package (`packages/shared`) for domain types. Configure its own `tsconfig.json` (strict) and Vitest. Add a trivial passing test.

All four checks (`lint`, `typecheck`, `unit`, `build`) must pass on this empty scaffold. Add a root `.gitignore` covering node_modules, dist, build, .gradle, and IDE files.",
      "requiredChecks": [
        "lint",
        "typecheck",
        "unit",
        "build"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-2",
      "title": "Domain model types, enums, and database schema",
      "description": "Define the complete domain model in both TypeScript shared types and Java entities, and create the full database schema.

**shared/ TypeScript types:**
- Enums: `PlanState` (DRAFT, LOCKED, RECONCILING, RECONCILED), `ChessPiece` (KING, QUEEN, ROOK, BISHOP, KNIGHT, PAWN), `CommitOutcome` (ACHIEVED, PARTIALLY_ACHIEVED, NOT_ACHIEVED, CANCELED), `RcdoNodeType` (RALLY_CRY, DEFINING_OBJECTIVE, OUTCOME), `RcdoNodeStatus` (DRAFT, ACTIVE, ARCHIVED), `TicketStatus` (BACKLOG, READY, IN_PROGRESS, BLOCKED, DONE, CANCELED), `ScopeChangeCategory` (COMMIT_ADDED, COMMIT_REMOVED, ESTIMATE_CHANGED, CHESS_PIECE_CHANGED, RCDO_CHANGED, PRIORITY_CHANGED), `CarryForwardReason` enum with controlled list.
- Interfaces: `WeeklyPlan`, `Commit`, `RcdoNode`, `Ticket`, `LockSnapshot`, `ScopeChangeEvent`, `ReconcileSnapshot`, `CarryForwardLink`, `AiSuggestion`, `ManagerComment`, `CapacityOverride`, `Notification`.
- Constants: `VALID_ESTIMATE_POINTS = [1, 2, 3, 5, 8]`, `DEFAULT_WEEKLY_BUDGET = 10`, `MAX_KING_PER_WEEK = 1`, `MAX_QUEEN_PER_WEEK = 2`, `SOFT_MAX_COMMITS = 8`, `PAWN_POINT_WARNING_THRESHOLD = 0.4`.
- Validation helpers with unit tests (e.g., isValidEstimate, isChessPieceWithinLimit).

**backend/ SQL migrations (Flyway):**
Create Flyway migrations under `src/main/resources/db/migration/` for all transactional tables from PRD §23: organization, team, user_account, team_membership, weekly_plan, commit, lock_snapshot_header, lock_snapshot_commit, scope_change_event, reconcile_snapshot_header, reconcile_snapshot_commit, carry_forward_link, work_item, work_item_status_history, work_item_comment, rcdo_node, rcdo_change_log, manager_comment, capacity_override, notification, notification_delivery, ai_suggestion, ai_feedback, audit_log. Include proper indexes, foreign keys, unique constraints (e.g., one plan per user per week), and check constraints (e.g., valid estimate points).

**backend/ Java entities:**
Create JPA entity classes under a `domain` package mirroring each table. Use Java enums matching the TypeScript enums. Add Jakarta Bean Validation annotations. Create repository interfaces (Spring Data JPA). Write unit tests for entity validation.",
      "requiredChecks": [
        "lint",
        "typecheck",
        "unit",
        "build"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-3",
      "title": "RCDO hierarchy management API",
      "description": "Implement the RCDO domain backend per PRD §15.

**Java service layer (`rcdo` module):**
- `RcdoService`: create, read, update, archive, move RCDO nodes.
- Hierarchy enforcement: Rally Cry has no parent; Defining Objective must have a Rally Cry parent; Outcome must have a Defining Objective parent.
- Status management: nodes start as Draft, can be activated (→ Active), and archived (→ Archived). Only Active nodes are valid for commit linkage.
- Archival rules: a node with active children cannot be archived; children must be archived or moved first.
- Move operation: re-parent a node within valid hierarchy constraints.
- `RcdoChangeLog` entries written on every create/update/archive/move.

**REST controller:**
- `POST /api/rcdo/nodes` — create node (validates parent type hierarchy)
- `GET /api/rcdo/nodes` — list nodes (filterable by type, status, parent, ownerTeam)
- `GET /api/rcdo/nodes/{id}` — get node with full ancestor path
- `PUT /api/rcdo/nodes/{id}` — update node fields
- `POST /api/rcdo/nodes/{id}/archive` — archive with child validation
- `POST /api/rcdo/nodes/{id}/move` — move to new parent
- `GET /api/rcdo/tree` — full hierarchy tree for selection UIs

**Commit linkage validation helper:**
- Validate that a commit's RCDO link targets an Outcome when active Outcomes exist under the parent DO, or a Defining Objective only when it has no active Outcomes. Rally Cries are never valid direct targets.

**Unit tests:** hierarchy enforcement, status transitions, archival rule violations, commit linkage validation, change log creation. Target high coverage of edge cases.",
      "requiredChecks": [
        "lint",
        "typecheck",
        "unit",
        "build"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-4",
      "title": "Weekly plan creation and commit CRUD API",
      "description": "Implement weekly plan and commit CRUD per PRD §13 FR-1, FR-2.

**WeeklyPlanService:**
- `getOrCreatePlan(userId, weekStartDate)`: returns existing plan or lazily creates a DRAFT plan. Enforces exactly one plan per user per week (unique constraint). Snapshots home team and configured capacity budget onto the plan.
- `getPlan(planId)`: returns plan with commits ordered by priority.
- `getMyCurrentWeekPlan(userId)`: resolves current week and delegates to getOrCreate.
- Week-start date calculation using configurable cadence (default: Monday).

**CommitService:**
- `createCommit(planId, commitData)`: validates plan is DRAFT, assigns next priority order. Validates chess piece limits across the plan (max 1 King, max 2 Queen). Validates estimate points ∈ {1,2,3,5,8}.
- `updateCommit(commitId, commitData)`: validates plan is DRAFT. Re-validates chess piece limits if piece changed.
- `deleteCommit(commitId)`: validates plan is DRAFT. Reorders remaining commits.
- `reorderCommits(planId, orderedCommitIds)`: validates plan is DRAFT. Sets new priority order.
- RCDO linkage validation: delegates to RcdoService helper (Outcome-first, DO fallback, no Rally Cry). If commit has a linked ticket with RCDO and commit RCDO is blank, default from ticket.
- Success criteria required for King and Queen chess pieces.

**REST controllers:**
- `GET/POST /api/plans` — list user plans, create/get-or-create plan
- `GET /api/plans/{id}` — plan detail with commits
- `POST /api/plans/{id}/commits` — create commit
- `PUT /api/plans/{id}/commits/{commitId}` — update commit
- `DELETE /api/plans/{id}/commits/{commitId}` — delete commit
- `PUT /api/plans/{id}/commits/reorder` — reorder commits

**Unit tests:** one-plan-per-user-per-week, chess piece limits, estimate validation, RCDO linkage rules, priority reorder, success criteria requirement for King/Queen.",
      "requiredChecks": [
        "lint",
        "typecheck",
        "unit",
        "build"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-5",
      "title": "Lock lifecycle and baseline snapshot API",
      "description": "Implement plan lock and baseline snapshot per PRD §13 FR-3 and §12.

**LockService:**
- `lockPlan(planId, userId)`: manual lock. Runs hard validation: all commits must have title, chess piece, priority order, primary RCDO link, and estimate points. King/Queen must have success criteria. Max 1 King, max 2 Queen. At least one commit required. If any hard validation fails, return errors and block lock.
- On successful validation: transition plan state DRAFT → LOCKED, capture baseline snapshot, set `lockedAt` timestamp, set `lockCompliance = MANUAL_ON_TIME` or `MANUAL_LATE` based on deadline.

**Baseline snapshot creation:**
- `LockSnapshotHeader`: references plan, stores snapshot timestamp, denormalized JSON payload of entire plan+commits+RCDO paths.
- `LockSnapshotCommit`: one row per commit with all field values at lock time, including denormalized RCDO path/labels as they existed at lock.
- Snapshot write must be transactional (all-or-nothing with state transition).

**Auto-lock job:**
- Scheduled background job that runs at configured lock cutoff time.
- Finds all DRAFT plans past their lock deadline.
- For each: transitions to LOCKED, captures baseline snapshot, marks `lockCompliance = SYSTEM_LOCKED`. If validation errors exist, additionally marks `system_locked_with_errors = true` and stores validation error list.
- Job must be idempotent: skip plans already LOCKED, use optimistic locking or compare-and-swap.

**REST endpoints:**
- `POST /api/plans/{id}/lock` — manual lock with validation response
- `GET /api/plans/{id}/lock-snapshot` — retrieve baseline snapshot

**Unit tests:** successful manual lock, validation failures blocking lock, auto-lock of expired drafts, system_locked_with_errors flag, snapshot immutability, idempotent re-runs, compliance flag computation, transactional integrity (snapshot + state change atomic).",
      "requiredChecks": [
        "lint",
        "typecheck",
        "unit",
        "build"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-6",
      "title": "Post-lock scope change and reconciliation API",
      "description": "Implement post-lock scope changes (PRD §13 FR-4) and reconciliation (PRD §13 FR-5).

**ScopeChangeService:**
- After lock, commit edits route through scope change events instead of direct mutation.
- `addPostLockCommit(planId, commitData, reason)`: creates commit + scope change event (COMMIT_ADDED).
- `removePostLockCommit(commitId, reason)`: marks commit canceled + scope change event (COMMIT_REMOVED).
- `editPostLockCommit(commitId, changes, reason)`: applies changes + scope change event per changed field (ESTIMATE_CHANGED, CHESS_PIECE_CHANGED, RCDO_CHANGED, PRIORITY_CHANGED). Each event stores before/after values.
- Reason text required for all material changes.
- `getChangeTimeline(planId)`: returns chronological scope change events.
- Manager exception detection: flag if change crosses threshold (e.g., King added/changed, total points increased >20%, etc.).

**ReconciliationService:**
- Automatic LOCKED → RECONCILING transition at configured reconcile-open time (background job, idempotent).
- `getReconciliationView(planId)`: returns baseline snapshot commits alongside current commit values, scope change summary, and linked ticket current statuses.
- `setCommitOutcome(commitId, outcome, notes)`: sets outcome (ACHIEVED, PARTIALLY_ACHIEVED, NOT_ACHIEVED, CANCELED). Notes required for PARTIALLY_ACHIEVED, NOT_ACHIEVED, and CANCELED.
- Auto-achieve: if a linked ticket has status DONE at reconciliation time, auto-set outcome to ACHIEVED.
- `submitReconciliation(planId)`: validates all commits have outcomes. Creates ReconcileSnapshotHeader + ReconcileSnapshotCommit rows (normalized + denormalized JSON). Transitions RECONCILING → RECONCILED. Sets compliance flags.

**REST endpoints:**
- `POST /api/plans/{id}/scope-changes` — add/remove/edit post-lock
- `GET /api/plans/{id}/scope-changes` — change timeline
- `GET /api/plans/{id}/reconcile` — reconciliation view
- `PUT /api/plans/{id}/commits/{commitId}/outcome` — set outcome
- `POST /api/plans/{id}/reconcile/submit` — submit reconciliation

**Unit tests:** scope change event creation with before/after, reason validation, auto-achieve from Done ticket, required notes for non-Achieved, reconcile snapshot creation, state transitions (LOCKED→RECONCILING→RECONCILED), idempotent reconcile-open job, manager exception flag generation.",
      "requiredChecks": [
        "lint",
        "typecheck",
        "unit",
        "build"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-7",
      "title": "Carry-forward and ticket/work item API",
      "description": "Implement carry-forward (PRD §13 FR-6) and native ticket model (PRD §14).

**CarryForwardService:**
- `carryForward(sourceCommitId, targetWeekStart, reason, reasonText?)`: creates a new commit in the target week's plan with provenance link to source. Copies title, description, chess piece, RCDO link, estimate. Sets `carryForwardSourceId`, increments `carryForwardStreak` (source streak + 1). New commit inserted at bottom of target plan's priority order.
- If target week plan doesn't exist, lazily create it.
- If target week plan is already LOCKED, add the carry-forward commit as a post-lock scope change event (COMMIT_ADDED with carry-forward reason).
- `CarryForwardLink` record: sourceCommitId, targetCommitId, reason, reasonText, createdAt.
- Carry-forward reason from controlled enum list plus optional free text.
- `getCarryForwardLineage(commitId)`: returns full chain of carry-forward ancestry.

**TicketService (WorkItemService):**
- CRUD: create, read, update, delete tickets.
- Fields: key/id (auto-generated), title, description, status, assignee, reporter, team, priority, optional estimate points, optional RCDO link, optional target week.
- Status workflow: BACKLOG → READY → IN_PROGRESS → BLOCKED → DONE → CANCELED. Validate transitions are legal.
- `WorkItemStatusHistory`: every status change timestamped and stored for duration-in-status reporting.
- Commit-ticket linking: a commit may link to zero or one ticket. In the same week, prevent the same assignee from linking the same ticket to multiple active commits.
- RCDO defaulting: when linking ticket to commit, if commit has no RCDO, default from ticket. If both have RCDO and they differ, flag a warning.

**REST endpoints:**
- `POST /api/plans/{id}/commits/{commitId}/carry-forward` — carry forward a commit
- `GET /api/commits/{id}/lineage` — carry-forward lineage
- `GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/tickets` — ticket CRUD
- `GET /api/tickets/{id}/history` — status history
- `PUT /api/plans/{id}/commits/{commitId}/link-ticket` — link ticket to commit

**Unit tests:** carry-forward creates new commit with provenance, streak incrementing, carry-forward into locked week, lineage chain traversal, ticket status transitions (valid and invalid), status history recording, same-week duplicate link prevention, RCDO defaulting and mismatch warning.",
      "requiredChecks": [
        "lint",
        "typecheck",
        "unit",
        "build"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-8",
      "title": "Team weekly view, manager review, and permissions API",
      "description": "Implement team view (PRD §13 FR-7, FR-8), manager features (PRD §20), and permissions (PRD §19).

**TeamWeeklyViewService:**
- `getTeamWeekView(teamId, weekStart)`: aggregates all weekly commits for team members for the selected week. Includes assigned tickets with no linked commit (uncommitted assigned work). Includes unassigned tickets targeted to the week (uncommitted unassigned work). De-duplication: tickets linked to commits must NOT appear in the uncommitted section.
- Roll-up by RCDO: aggregate points and commit counts by Rally Cry / DO / Outcome.
- Chess distribution: count and points per chess piece for the team.
- Compliance summary: lock compliance, reconcile compliance per team member.

**ManagerReviewService:**
- `getExceptionQueue(teamId, weekStart)`: returns exceptions ordered by severity (King/Queen first, then risk level). Exception types: missed/auto lock, missed reconcile, over-budget plans (points > capacity budget), repeated carry-forward (streak >= 2), post-lock major scope increases (>20% point increase), added/changed King after lock, high scope volatility (>3 scope change events).
- `addComment(targetType, targetId, managerId, text)`: plan-level or commit-level comments. Only direct managers may comment on direct reports.
- `setCapacityOverride(userId, weekStart, overridePoints, reason)`: manager sets custom capacity budget for a user for a specific week.
- `resolveException(exceptionId, resolution)`: mark exception as reviewed/resolved.

**Permission/RBAC middleware:**
- Role enum: IC, MANAGER, ADMIN.
- `AuthorizationService` enforces: IC can only access own plans/commits; Manager can access direct reports' full detail and indirect reports' aggregates; Admin can access all.
- Peer visibility: same-team peers see only current and next week basic commit detail (title, owner, priority, chess piece, RCDO, ticket link, state). Hidden: AI risk flags, historical patterns, manager comments, reconcile notes.
- Row-level filtering applied at service layer (not just frontend).
- Privacy filter on API responses: strip hidden fields based on caller role and relationship.

**REST endpoints:**
- `GET /api/teams/{id}/week/{weekStart}` — team weekly view
- `GET /api/teams/{id}/week/{weekStart}/exceptions` — exception queue
- `POST /api/comments` — add comment
- `PUT /api/capacity-overrides` — set capacity override
- `PUT /api/exceptions/{id}/resolve` — resolve exception

**Unit tests:** team view aggregation with de-duplication, RCDO rollup accuracy, exception detection for each type, comment authorization (only direct manager), capacity override application, peer visibility filtering, manager vs IC access boundaries, admin access, privacy field stripping.",
      "requiredChecks": [
        "lint",
        "typecheck",
        "unit",
        "build"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-9",
      "title": "Frontend micro-frontend setup and routing",
      "description": "Set up the frontend React application as a Module Federation remote per PRD §22.

**Vite + Module Federation:**
- Configure `@originjs/vite-plugin-federation` (or `@module-federation/vite`) to expose the app as a remote module named `weeklyCommit`.
- Expose routes entry point for host consumption.
- Configure shared dependencies (react, react-dom, react-router-dom).

**Host integration contract:**
- Define TypeScript interfaces in shared/ for the host bridge: `HostContext` (authenticatedUser, currentTeam, managerChain, environment, featureFlags), `DesignTokens`, `NotificationBridge`, `TelemetryCallback`, `NavigationBridge`.
- Create a `HostProvider` React context that provides host bridge data to the app tree.
- Create a mock host provider for standalone development.

**Routing:**
- React Router v6 with 5 primary routes under a base path:
  - `/weekly/my-week` — My Week (default landing)
  - `/weekly/reconcile/:planId?` — Reconcile view
  - `/weekly/team/:teamId?` — Team Week dashboard
  - `/weekly/tickets` — Tickets list/detail
  - `/weekly/rcdos` — RCDO management
- Lazy-loaded route components with Suspense boundaries.

**API client:**
- Typed API client using fetch with interceptors for auth token injection from host context.
- Request/response types imported from shared/ package.
- Error handling wrapper with typed error responses.
- Loading and error state hooks (useQuery pattern or lightweight data fetching).

**App shell:**
- Layout component with navigation sidebar listing all 5 routes.
- Header with user info from host context, week selector, and notification bell icon.
- Design token integration (CSS custom properties from host or defaults).
- Responsive layout (desktop-first, not mobile-first per PRD §26).

**Component tests:** HostProvider renders with mock data, router navigates between all 5 routes, API client handles success and error responses, layout renders navigation items.",
      "requiredChecks": [
        "lint",
        "typecheck",
        "unit",
        "build"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-10",
      "title": "RCDO management UI",
      "description": "Build the RCDO hierarchy management frontend per PRD §10 and §15.

**RcdoTreeView component:**
- Renders the full RCDO hierarchy as an expandable tree: Rally Cries → Defining Objectives → Outcomes.
- Each node shows: title, status badge (Draft/Active/Archived), owner team, node type icon.
- Expand/collapse nodes. Expand all / collapse all controls.
- Filter tree by status (show active only, show all, show archived).
- Search within tree by title.

**RcdoNodeForm component:**
- Create and edit form for RCDO nodes.
- Fields: title (required), description, owner team (selector), owner user (optional selector), status, target metric (Outcomes only).
- Parent selector: when creating a DO, must select a Rally Cry parent; when creating an Outcome, must select a DO parent.
- Validation: title required, parent type must match hierarchy rules.

**RCDO actions:**
- Create node: opens form in create mode, selects parent based on context.
- Edit node: opens form in edit mode with current values.
- Archive node: confirmation dialog explaining impact; disabled with message if node has active children.
- Move node: re-parent dialog showing valid target parents.
- Permission-based visibility: only Admin sees Rally Cry create/edit; Managers see DO/Outcome within their team branches; ICs see read-only view.

**RCDO page layout:**
- Left panel: tree view with filters.
- Right panel: selected node detail / form.
- Breadcrumb showing full path (Rally Cry > DO > Outcome).

**Component tests:** tree renders hierarchy correctly, create form validates required fields, archive blocked with active children, permission-based action hiding, search filters tree nodes, breadcrumb updates on selection.",
      "requiredChecks": [
        "lint",
        "typecheck",
        "unit",
        "build"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-11",
      "title": "My Week page with commit list and capacity meter",
      "description": "Build the My Week page per PRD §10 (My Week surface) and §13 FR-2.

**MyWeekPage component:**
- Displays current week plan or upcoming draft (auto-selects based on lifecycle stage).
- Week selector to navigate between weeks.
- Plan state badge (DRAFT, LOCKED, RECONCILING, RECONCILED) with compliance indicator.
- Lock/Reconcile action buttons (contextual based on plan state).

**CommitList component:**
- Ordered list of commits showing: priority rank, chess piece icon, title, RCDO path, estimate points, linked ticket key, status.
- Drag-and-drop reorder using a library (e.g., @dnd-kit/sortable). Keyboard-accessible reorder alternative: move-up/move-down buttons per commit (WCAG 2.1 AA).
- Inline expand to show description, success criteria, provenance info if carried forward.
- Edit and delete actions per commit (only in DRAFT state).

**CommitForm component (modal or panel):**
- Fields: title (required), description (textarea), chess piece (radio/select with piece icons and descriptions), estimate points (segmented control: 1/2/3/5/8), primary RCDO link (hierarchical selector using RcdoTreeView in picker mode), success criteria (textarea, required badge for King/Queen), linked ticket (search/select).
- Real-time validation: required fields highlighted, chess piece limits checked (show 'Max 1 King already used' warning), RCDO linkage rules enforced.
- Carry-forward provenance banner if commit was carried forward (shows source week and streak count).

**CapacityMeter component:**
- Visual bar/gauge showing committed points vs weekly budget (default 10).
- Color-coded: green (under budget), yellow (near budget), red (over budget).
- Breakdown by chess piece type.
- Override indicator if manager has set custom capacity.

**Soft warnings panel:**
- Warning if >8 active commits in the plan.
- Warning if >40% of total points are Pawn commits.
- These are UI warnings only, not lock blockers.

**Component tests:** commit list renders in priority order, drag-drop reorder updates priority, commit form validates all required fields, chess piece limit enforced in UI, capacity meter shows correct totals, soft warnings appear at thresholds, RCDO selector enforces linkage rules, keyboard reorder works.",

      "id": "step-12",
      "title": "Lock flow and reconcile view UI",
      "description": "Build the lock and reconciliation flows per PRD §10, §11 (Flows 1-4), §13 FR-3/FR-5/FR-6.

**Lock flow (within My Week page):**
- Pre-lock validation panel: runs validation and displays results as two sections: Hard Errors (block lock) and Soft Warnings (informational). Hard errors: missing required fields, chess piece limit violations. Soft warnings: AI lint results (added in later step, placeholder panel here).
- Lock confirmation dialog: shows summary of commits being locked, total points, RCDO coverage. Confirm/cancel buttons.
- On lock success: show baseline captured confirmation, plan transitions to LOCKED state in UI.
- Auto-lock status: if plan was auto-locked, show banner indicating system lock with any validation errors that existed.

**Post-lock edit flow (within My Week page, LOCKED state):**
- Edit/add/remove commit actions available but route through scope change dialog.
- ScopeChangeDialog: requires reason category (from ScopeChangeCategory enum) and reason text. Shows before/after comparison for edits.
- Change timeline view: chronological list of all scope changes since lock.

**ReconcilePage component:**
- Two-column comparison: baseline snapshot (left) vs current state (right) for each commit.
- Per commit: baseline values (chess piece, estimate, RCDO, title) vs current values. Highlight changes in red/green.
- Scope change summary section: list of all post-lock changes grouped by type.
- Linked ticket evidence: for each commit with a linked ticket, show ticket current status and status history summary.
- Outcome selector per commit: ACHIEVED (green), PARTIALLY_ACHIEVED (yellow), NOT_ACHIEVED (red), CANCELED (gray). Auto-populated to ACHIEVED if linked ticket is Done.
- Reconcile notes: textarea per commit, required for PARTIALLY_ACHIEVED, NOT_ACHIEVED, CANCELED (validation enforced).
- Carry-forward section: for NOT_ACHIEVED and PARTIALLY_ACHIEVED commits, offer carry-forward checkbox. CarryForwardDialog: select reason from controlled list, optional text, confirm target week.
- Submit reconciliation button: validates all commits have outcomes and required notes. Shows confirmation summary.

**Component tests:** pre-lock validation shows errors and warnings, lock blocked on hard errors, scope change dialog captures reason, reconcile view renders baseline vs actual, outcome selector works, required notes enforced, carry-forward dialog creates request, auto-achieve shown for Done tickets.",

      "id": "step-13",
      "title": "Team Week dashboard UI",
      "description": "Build the Team Week manager dashboard per PRD §10, §20.

**TeamWeekPage component:**
- Team selector (for managers with multiple teams) and week selector.
- Tabbed or sectioned layout with all dashboard sections.

**OverviewSection:**
- Compliance cards: lock compliance (X/Y locked, % on-time), reconcile compliance (X/Y reconciled, % on-time).
- Points summary: total planned points, total achieved points, delta.
- Open exceptions count with severity breakdown.

**ByPersonSection:**
- Table/list of direct reports with columns: name, plan state, points committed/budget, chess piece summary (icons), risk flags (icons), carry-forward streak count, major post-lock changes indicator.
- Expandable rows to show individual commit lists.
- Click through to view full plan detail for a direct report.

**ByRcdoSection:**
- Tree or grouped table: Rally Cry → DO → Outcome with planned points, achieved points, commit count per branch.
- Coverage gap highlighting: RCDOs with no commits shown distinctly.
- Planned vs achieved bar per branch.

**ChessDistributionSection:**
- Stacked bar or pie chart: team's commit and point distribution across King/Queen/Rook/Bishop/Knight/Pawn.
- Highlight concentration of critical work (King/Queen allocation).

**UncommittedWorkSection:**
- Two sub-lists: assigned tickets with no linked commit; unassigned tickets targeted to the week.
- Ticket details: key, title, assignee (or 'Unassigned'), status, target week.
- Quick-assign action for unassigned tickets.

**ExceptionQueueSection:**
- Filterable list of exceptions sorted by severity (King/Queen first).
- Exception types: missed lock, missed reconcile, over-budget, added/changed King after lock, repeated carry-forward, high scope volatility.
- Each exception shows: user, type, detail, created date, resolution status.
- Resolve action: mark as reviewed with optional note.
- Comment action: add plan-level or commit-level comment.

**Component tests:** overview cards compute correct aggregates, by-person table renders direct reports, RCDO rollup groups correctly, chess distribution renders accurately, uncommitted work shows correct tickets without double-counting, exception queue sorts by severity, resolve and comment actions work.",

    {
      "id": "step-14",
      "title": "Tickets UI and search/filter/history views",
      "description": "Build the Tickets page and cross-cutting search/filter/history per PRD §10, §14, §13 FR-9.

**TicketsPage:**
- TicketListView: table of tickets with columns: key, title, status, assignee, team, priority, RCDO, target week. Sortable columns. Pagination.
- Filters: status, assignee, team, RCDO, target week, priority. Persistent filter state in URL params.
- Create ticket button: opens TicketForm.
- TicketForm: title, description, status (default Backlog), assignee, reporter (default current user), team, priority, estimate points (optional), RCDO link (optional), target week (optional).
- TicketDetailView: full ticket detail with status history timeline, linked commits list, RCDO path, comments. Status transition buttons (valid next-status options based on current status). Assignment change.
- Create-from-commit: action on a commit that creates a ticket pre-populated from commit title/description/RCDO/estimate.

**Search and Filter bar (reusable component):**
- Global filter bar used across pages. Filter dimensions: week (date picker), user (search/select), team (select), RCDO (hierarchical picker), chess piece (multi-select), plan state (multi-select), risk flag (multi-select).
- Filters applied via URL query params for shareability.
- Clear all / save filter preset.

**PlanHistoryView (within My Week):**
- Week-by-week history list for the current user.
- Per week: plan state, compliance, commit count, total points planned/achieved, carry-forward count.
- Click to expand full plan detail for any historical week.

**CarryForwardLineageView:**
- Visualization of carry-forward chain: shows original commit → carried week 1 → carried week 2 → etc.
- Each node shows: week, outcome, streak count.
- Accessible from any commit that has carry-forward provenance.

**TeamHistoryView (within Team Week):**
- Weekly trend table for the team: compliance rate, planned vs achieved points, carry-forward rate, exception count per week.
- Week-over-week comparison.

**Component tests:** ticket list renders with filters, ticket form validates required fields, status transitions show only valid options, create-from-commit pre-fills data, filter bar applies and clears correctly, plan history shows weeks in order, carry-forward lineage renders chain, team history computes trends.",

    {
      "id": "step-15",
      "title": "Notifications backend and frontend panel",
      "description": "Implement the notifications system per PRD §18.

**Backend NotificationService:**
- `NotificationEvent` enum covering all default events: DRAFT_WINDOW_OPENED, LOCK_DUE_REMINDER, AUTO_LOCK_OCCURRED, RECONCILIATION_OPENED, RECONCILIATION_DUE_REMINDER, REPEATED_CARRY_FORWARD_REMINDER, MANAGER_EXCEPTION_DIGEST, UNASSIGNED_TICKET_CREATED, CRITICAL_TICKET_BLOCKED.
- `createNotification(userId, event, payload, priority)`: stores notification in DB.
- Priority behavior: KING_EXCEPTION → immediate (high priority), QUEEN_ROOK_EXCEPTION → same-day digest (medium), BISHOP_KNIGHT_PAWN → daily digest (low).
- Frequency controls: max 2 direct reminders per user per task per day. Low-priority batched into daily digest.
- Business hours check: respect team local time.

**Notification triggers (integrated into lifecycle services):**
- Hook into auto-lock job → AUTO_LOCK_OCCURRED notification to user + MANAGER_EXCEPTION_DIGEST to manager.
- Hook into reconcile-open job → RECONCILIATION_OPENED notification.
- Hook into carry-forward → check streak, if >= 2 send REPEATED_CARRY_FORWARD_REMINDER.
- Hook into scope change → if King changed, immediate MANAGER notification.
- Hook into ticket status → if blocked and linked to King/Queen, CRITICAL_TICKET_BLOCKED.

**NotificationDelivery tracking:**
- Store delivery records: notificationId, channel (IN_APP, EMAIL), deliveredAt, readAt.
- Mark-as-read endpoint.

**Email digest preparation:**
- `EmailDigestService`: aggregates pending notifications by user and priority. Prepares digest payload (actual email sending via host or external service — interface only in v1).

**Frontend NotificationPanel:**
- Bell icon in header with unread count badge.
- Dropdown panel showing recent notifications grouped by priority.
- Mark-as-read on click. Mark-all-read action.
- Click notification to navigate to relevant resource (plan, commit, ticket, team view).

**REST endpoints:**
- `GET /api/notifications` — list user's notifications (paginated, filterable by read/unread)
- `PUT /api/notifications/{id}/read` — mark as read
- `PUT /api/notifications/read-all` — mark all as read

**Unit tests:** notification creation with correct priority, frequency limiting (no >2 per task per day), lifecycle hook triggers correct events, delivery tracking records, digest aggregation groups correctly, frontend panel renders notifications, unread count badge updates.",

    {
      "id": "step-16",
      "title": "AI provider abstraction and commit assistance",
      "description": "Implement the AI foundation and commit-level assistance per PRD §17 (capabilities 1-3).

**AI provider abstraction (backend):**
- `AiProvider` interface: `generateSuggestion(context: AiContext): Promise<AiSuggestionResult>`. Context includes commit data, plan data, RCDO tree, historical patterns.
- `AiProviderRegistry`: registers providers, selects active provider. Supports disabling AI entirely via feature flag.
- Graceful degradation: if provider unavailable or disabled, all AI endpoints return empty suggestions with `aiAvailable: false` flag. Core workflow unaffected.
- Default implementation: stub provider for development; production provider pluggable.

**AI suggestion storage:**
- `AiSuggestion` records: id, userId, suggestionType, context hash, suggestion payload, rationale text, modelVersion, createdAt.
- `AiFeedback` records: suggestionId, action (ACCEPTED, DISMISSED, EDITED), timestamp.
- Every suggestion stores rationale explaining why it was generated.

**Commit drafting assistance (capability 1):**
- `POST /api/ai/commit-draft-assist`: given commit title/description, returns suggestions for: improved title wording, concise description, success criteria (especially for King/Queen), estimate points based on similar past commits.
- Suggestions returned as editable proposals, never auto-applied.
- Frontend integration: 'AI Assist' button on CommitForm. Shows suggestion panel with accept/edit/dismiss per suggestion.

**Commit quality linting (capability 2):**
- `POST /api/ai/commit-lint`: given plan with all commits, returns lint results.
- Checks: vague titles (too short, too generic), missing success criteria for King/Queen, parent-level RCDO linkage when leaf exists, duplicate/near-duplicate titles, estimate inconsistency (similar work different estimates), over-fragmented plan (>8 commits).
- Results clearly separated into: `hardValidation` (blocks lock, same as lock validation) and `softGuidance` (AI suggestions, informational only).
- Frontend integration: AI Lint Panel on My Week page. Shows lint results with severity icons. Runs on save and pre-lock.

**RCDO link suggestions (capability 3):**
- `POST /api/ai/rcdo-suggest`: given commit text and optional ticket data, suggests primary RCDO with confidence score and rationale.
- Only surface suggestions above confidence threshold (configurable, default 0.7).
- Rationale examples: 'keyword match with Outcome X', 'linked ticket is tagged to Outcome Y', 'similar commit last week linked to Outcome Z'.
- Never auto-link. Frontend shows suggestion with accept/dismiss in RCDO selector.

**Unit tests:** provider abstraction with graceful degradation, suggestion storage and retrieval, feedback recording, draft assistance returns structured suggestions, lint distinguishes hard vs soft, RCDO suggestion respects confidence threshold, AI unavailable doesn't break workflows, rationale attached to every suggestion.",
      "requiredChecks": [
        "lint",
        "typecheck",
        "unit",
        "build"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-17",
      "title": "AI risk detection and reconciliation assistance",
      "description": "Implement AI risk signals and reconciliation assistance per PRD §17 (capabilities 4-6).

**Risk detection service (capability 4):**
- `RiskDetectionService`: computes risk signals per plan.
- Runs on plan lock and daily for LOCKED plans (background job).
- v1 risk signals with rationale:
  - Overcommit: committed points > budget AND > user's historical average completed points. Rationale: 'X points committed vs Y budget, historical average completion is Z points.'
  - Undercommit: committed points < 50% of budget with no capacity override. Rationale: 'Only X of Y budget points committed.'
  - Repeated carry-forward: commit has carry-forward streak >= 2. Rationale: 'This work has been carried forward N consecutive weeks from [original date].'
  - Blocked critical: King/Queen commit linked to a ticket in BLOCKED status for >2 business days. Rationale: 'King commit [title] linked to blocked ticket [key] for N days.'
  - Scope volatility: >3 scope change events on a locked plan. Rationale: 'N scope changes recorded since lock, including [summary].'
- Risk signals stored in `ai_suggestion` table with type RISK_SIGNAL.
- Exposed via `GET /api/plans/{id}/risk-signals`.

**Reconciliation assistance (capability 5):**
- `POST /api/ai/reconcile-assist`: given plan in RECONCILING state, returns:
  - Likely outcomes for unlinked commits based on notes and activity patterns.
  - Draft summary of what changed after lock (generated from scope change events).
  - Carry-forward recommendations with rationale for incomplete work.
- All suggestions are proposals requiring user acceptance.
- Frontend integration: 'AI Assist' panel on ReconcilePage. Shows suggestions per commit and overall summary. Accept/edit/dismiss controls.

**Manager summary (capability 6):**
- `GET /api/teams/{id}/week/{weekStart}/ai-summary`: generates team summary citing:
  - Top RCDO branches receiving effort (with commit/point counts).
  - Unresolved exceptions (linked to exception queue items).
  - Repeated carry-forward patterns (names and streaks).
  - Critical blocked items (linked to tickets).
- Summary must cite underlying objects (IDs/links), not be a black-box narrative.
- Frontend: summary card at top of Team Week dashboard (collapsible, dismissible).

**Frontend risk displays:**
- Risk flag icons on commits and plans in My Week and Team Week.
- Risk detail tooltip/popover showing rationale text.
- Risk flags hidden from peers (only visible to plan owner and manager per privacy rules).

**Unit tests:** each risk signal type computed correctly with rationale, daily risk job processes LOCKED plans, reconcile assistance produces structured suggestions, manager summary cites source objects, risk flags respect privacy (hidden from peers), AI down returns empty risk signals without error.",

        "lint",

    {
      "id": "step-18",
      "title": "Derived read models and operational reporting",
      "description": "Implement derived read models and reporting per PRD §21, §23.

**Derived tables (Flyway migrations):**
- `user_week_fact`: userId, weekStart, planState, lockCompliance, reconcileCompliance, totalPlannedPoints, totalAchievedPoints, commitCount, carryForwardCount, scopeChangeCount, kingCount, queenCount.
- `team_week_rollup`: teamId, weekStart, memberCount, lockedCount, reconciledCount, totalPlannedPoints, totalAchievedPoints, exceptionCount, avgCarryForwardRate.
- `rcdo_week_rollup`: rcdoNodeId, weekStart, plannedPoints, achievedPoints, commitCount, teamContributionBreakdown (JSON).
- `compliance_fact`: userId, weekStart, lockOnTime, lockLate, autoLocked, reconcileOnTime, reconcileLate, reconcileMissed.
- `carry_forward_fact`: commitId, sourceWeek, currentWeek, streakLength, rcdoNodeId, chessPiece.

**Refresh service:**
- `ReadModelRefreshService`: recomputes derived tables.
- Event-driven refresh: triggered by lock, reconcile, scope change, carry-forward events.
- Scheduled refresh: runs every 5 minutes for current-week data (fallback if events missed).
- Idempotent: safe to re-run without creating duplicates.

**Reporting API:**
- `GET /api/reports/compliance?teamId=&weekStart=&weekEnd=` — lock/reconcile compliance by team/week.
- `GET /api/reports/planned-vs-achieved?teamId=&weekStart=&weekEnd=` — points comparison.
- `GET /api/reports/carry-forward?teamId=&weekStart=&weekEnd=` — carry-forward rates and streaks.
- `GET /api/reports/chess-distribution?teamId=&weekStart=` — chess piece distribution.
- `GET /api/reports/scope-changes?teamId=&weekStart=&weekEnd=` — scope change volume.
- `GET /api/reports/rcdo-coverage?rcdoNodeId=&weekStart=&weekEnd=` — RCDO branch effort.
- `GET /api/reports/ai-acceptance` — AI suggestion acceptance rates.
- `GET /api/reports/exception-aging?teamId=` — unresolved exception age.
- All reports query derived tables, not transactional tables (P95 < 1.5s target).

**Manager dashboard optimization:**
- Team Week dashboard components updated to query from derived read model endpoints instead of live aggregation.
- Verify P95 dashboard query under 1.5 seconds.

**Unit tests:** derived table refresh computes correct aggregates, idempotent re-runs produce same results, event-driven refresh triggered on lifecycle events, report endpoints return correctly filtered data, dashboard queries use read models.",
      "requiredChecks": [
        "lint",
        "typecheck",
        "unit",
        "build"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-19",
      "title": "Configuration management and admin settings",
      "description": "Implement cadence configuration and admin settings per PRD §13 FR-10.

**Configuration model:**
- `OrgConfig` table: orgId, default week cadence fields (weekStartDay, draftOpenOffset, lockDueOffset, reconcileOpenOffset, reconcileDueOffset), defaultWeeklyBudget, timezone.
- `TeamConfigOverride` table: teamId, overridden cadence fields (null = use org default), timezone override.
- `CapacityOverride` (already exists from step 8): per-user per-week override.

**ConfigurationService:**
- `getEffectiveConfig(teamId)`: merges org defaults with team overrides. Returns resolved cadence deadlines for any given week.
- `getEffectiveCapacity(userId, weekStart)`: returns user capacity (override > team default > org default, default 10).
- `updateOrgConfig(config)`: admin-only.
- `updateTeamConfig(teamId, overrides)`: admin or team manager.
- Default cadence per PRD §12: planning week Mon-Fri, draft opens Friday 12:00, reconcile opens Friday 17:00, reconcile due Monday 10:00, lock due Monday 12:00.

**Integration with lifecycle services:**
- Auto-lock job reads effective config to determine cutoff time per team.
- Reconcile-open job reads effective config to determine transition time.
- Plan creation uses effective capacity for budget snapshot.

**Admin settings UI (frontend):**
- Admin-only page (accessible from a settings gear icon for Admin role).
- Org-level config form: week start day, deadline offsets, default budget.
- Team-level overrides: list of teams with override indicators, edit form per team.
- Per-user capacity overrides: searchable user list with current/override capacity display.

**REST endpoints:**
- `GET /api/config/org` — org config
- `PUT /api/config/org` — update org config (admin only)
- `GET /api/config/teams/{id}` — team config with effective values
- `PUT /api/config/teams/{id}` — update team override
- `GET /api/config/capacity/{userId}?week=` — effective capacity

**Unit tests:** effective config merges org + team correctly, capacity resolution chain (override > team > org > default), lifecycle jobs use per-team deadlines, admin-only access enforced, config changes don't affect already-locked plans.",
      "requiredChecks": [
        "lint",
        "typecheck",
        "unit",
        "build"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-20",
      "title": "Audit logging, accessibility, and integration polish",
      "description": "Implement comprehensive audit logging (PRD §13 FR-11), accessibility (PRD §24), and final integration polish.

**Audit logging (backend):**
- `AuditLogService`: records all auditable events with actor, action, target, timestamp, before/after payload.
- Auditable events: plan state transitions (create, lock, auto-lock, reconcile-open, reconcile-submit), commit CRUD, scope change events, carry-forward actions, RCDO create/update/archive/move, ticket status changes, capacity overrides, manager comments, AI suggestion create/feedback, admin config changes.
- `audit_log` table: id, actorId, actorRole, action enum, targetType, targetId, beforePayload (JSON), afterPayload (JSON), timestamp, ipAddress.
- Audit records are append-only and immutable.
- `GET /api/audit-log?targetType=&targetId=&actorId=&action=&from=&to=` — query audit log (admin only, or scoped to own actions for IC/manager).

**Snapshot immutability verification:**
- Add integrity checks: lock snapshots and reconcile snapshots cannot be modified after creation.
- Database-level: no UPDATE/DELETE permissions on snapshot tables for application user (or application-level write-once enforcement).

**Idempotency verification:**
- Review all lifecycle jobs (auto-lock, reconcile-open, read-model refresh) for idempotency.
- Add guards: check current state before transition, use optimistic locking, verify no duplicate snapshots or carry-forward links on retry.

**Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA):**
- Keyboard navigation: all interactive elements focusable and operable via keyboard.
- Commit reorder: ensure keyboard alternative (move-up/move-down buttons) works correctly with screen readers.
- ARIA labels on all icon-only buttons, status badges, and interactive elements.
- Color independence: chess piece types, plan states, risk flags, and outcomes distinguishable without color alone (icons, text labels, patterns).
- Focus management: modal dialogs trap focus, return focus on close.
- Form error announcements: validation errors announced to screen readers.

**Integration verification:**
- End-to-end flow tests (unit test level): create plan → add commits → lock → scope change → reconcile → carry-forward. Verify snapshots, events, and state transitions.
- Verify permission boundaries: IC cannot access other's plans, peer sees only basic detail, manager sees full detail for directs.
- Verify AI degradation: disable AI provider, confirm all core flows complete successfully.
- Performance spot-checks: verify derived read model queries are used for dashboard endpoints.

**Unit tests:** audit log records all event types with correct payloads, snapshot immutability enforced, lifecycle job idempotency (double-run produces no duplicates), accessibility attributes present on key components, end-to-end flow produces correct state transitions and snapshots, permission boundaries enforced.",
      "requiredChecks": [
        "lint",
        "typecheck",
        "unit",
        "build"
      ]
    }
  ]
}

### 1. TicketStatus Enum Mismatch

The frontend expects a priority field on tickets with values like `CRITICAL`, `HIGH`, `MEDIUM`, `LOW`, but the backend's WorkItem entity doesn't have this field at all.

The frontend needs rich ticket responses that include nested arrays like `statusHistory` and `linkedCommits` along with fields like `priority` and `key`, but the backend returns a flat structure without these relationships.

The frontend updates ticket status through a dedicated endpoint that accepts the new status and the user making the change, which is properly separated from general ticket updates.

      "description": "Implement plan lock and baseline snapshot per PRD §13 FR-3 and §12.

**LockService:**
- `lockPlan(planId, userId)`: manual lock. Runs hard validation: all commits must have title, chess piece, priority order, primary RCDO link, and estimate points. King/Queen must have success criteria. Max 1 King, max 2 Queen. At least one commit required. If any hard validation fails, return errors and block lock.
- On successful validation: transition plan state DRAFT → LOCKED, capture baseline snapshot, set `lockedAt` timestamp, set `lockCompliance = MANUAL_ON_TIME` or `MANUAL_LATE` based on deadline.

**Baseline snapshot creation:**
- `LockSnapshotHeader`: references plan, stores snapshot timestamp, denormalized JSON payload of entire plan+commits+RCDO paths.
- `LockSnapshotCommit`: one row per commit with all field values at lock time, including denormalized RCDO path/labels as they existed at lock.
- Snapshot write must be transactional (all-or-nothing with state transition).

**Auto-lock job:**
- Scheduled background job that runs at configured lock cutoff time.
- Finds all DRAFT plans past their lock deadline.
- For each: transitions to LOCKED, captures baseline snapshot, marks `lockCompliance = SYSTEM_LOCKED`. If validation errors exist, additionally marks `system_locked_with_errors = true` and stores validation error list.
- Job must be idempotent: skip plans already LOCKED, use optimistic locking or compare-and-swap.

**REST endpoints:**
- `POST /api/plans/{id}/lock` — manual lock with validation response
- `GET /api/plans/{id}/lock-snapshot` — retrieve baseline snapshot

**Unit tests:** successful manual lock, validation failures blocking lock, auto-lock of expired drafts, system_locked_with_errors flag, snapshot immutability, idempotent re-runs, compliance flag computation, transactional integrity (snapshot + state change atomic).",
